root_text.an1.71:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā akusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, kalyāṇamittatā.
root_text.an1.71:1.2: Kalyāṇamittassa, bhikkhave, anuppannā ceva kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca akusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.
root_text.an1.72:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā kusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, anuyogo akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ, ananuyogo kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ.
root_text.an1.72:1.2: Anuyogā, bhikkhave, akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ, ananuyogā kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppannā ceva akusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca kusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.
root_text.an1.73:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā vā akusalā dhammā parihāyanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, anuyogo kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ, ananuyogo akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ.
root_text.an1.73:1.2: Anuyogā, bhikkhave, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ, ananuyogā akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppannā ceva kusalā dhammā uppajjanti uppannā ca akusalā dhammā parihāyantī”ti.
root_text.an1.74:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā bojjhaṅgā nuppajjanti uppannā vā bojjhaṅgā na bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, ayonisomanasikāro.
root_text.an1.74:1.2: Ayoniso, bhikkhave, manasi karoto anuppannā ceva bojjhaṅgā nuppajjanti uppannā ca bojjhaṅgā na bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchantī”ti.
root_text.an1.75:1.1: “Nāhaṁ, bhikkhave, aññaṁ ekadhammampi samanupassāmi yena anuppannā vā bojjhaṅgā uppajjanti uppannā vā bojjhaṅgā bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchanti yathayidaṁ, bhikkhave, yonisomanasikāro.
root_text.an1.75:1.2: Yoniso, bhikkhave, manasi karoto anuppannā ceva bojjhaṅgā uppajjanti uppannā ca bojjhaṅgā bhāvanāpāripūriṁ gacchantī”ti.
root_text.an1.76:1.2: Etaṁ patikiṭṭhaṁ, bhikkhave, parihānīnaṁ yadidaṁ paññāparihānī”ti.
root_text.an1.78:1.2: Etaṁ patikiṭṭhaṁ, bhikkhave, parihānīnaṁ yadidaṁ paññāparihānī”ti.
root_text.an1.80:1.2: Etaṁ patikiṭṭhaṁ, bhikkhave, parihānīnaṁ yadidaṁ paññāparihānī”ti.

translation_text.an1.71:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to skillful qualities, or makes unskillful qualities decline like good friends.
translation_text.an1.71:1.2: When you have good friends, skillful qualities arise and unskillful qualities decline.”
translation_text.an1.72:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to unskillful qualities, or makes skillful qualities decline like pursuing bad habits and not good habits.
translation_text.an1.72:1.2: When you pursue bad habits and not good habits, unskillful qualities arise and skillful qualities decline.”
translation_text.an1.73:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to skillful qualities, or makes unskillful qualities decline like pursuing good habits and not bad habits.
translation_text.an1.73:1.2: When you pursue good habits and not bad habits, skillful qualities arise and unskillful qualities decline.”
translation_text.an1.74:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that prevents the awakening factors from arising, or, if they’ve already arisen, prevents them from being fully developed like irrational application of mind.
translation_text.an1.74:1.2: When you apply the mind irrationally, the awakening factors don’t arise, or, if they’ve already arisen, they’re not fully developed.”
translation_text.an1.75:1.1: “Mendicants, I do not see a single thing that gives rise to the awakening factors, or, if they’ve already arisen, fully develops them like rational application of mind.
translation_text.an1.75:1.2: When you apply the mind rationally, the awakening factors arise, or, if they’ve already arisen, they’re fully developed.”
translation_text.an1.76:1.1: “Loss of relatives, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.76:1.2: Wisdom is the worst thing to lose.”
translation_text.an1.77:1.1: “Growth of relatives, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.77:1.2: Wisdom is the best thing to grow.
translation_text.an1.77:1.3: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an1.78:1.1: “Loss of wealth, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.78:1.2: Wisdom is the worst thing to lose.”
translation_text.an1.79:1.1: “Growth of wealth, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.79:1.2: Wisdom is the best thing to grow.
translation_text.an1.79:1.3: So you should train like this:
translation_text.an1.80:1.1: “Loss of fame, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.80:1.2: Wisdom is the worst thing to lose.”
translation_text.an1.81:1.1: “Growth of fame, mendicants, is a small thing.
translation_text.an1.81:1.2: Wisdom is the best thing to grow.
translation_text.an1.81:1.3: So you should train like this:
